patches.find: The per-WIN Author's Self-Rating block and this subsection together form a testable monitor. If the author updates the homepage to reconcile the 10 / 9 / 1 count drift, we show it resolved. If he classifies more claims under the 5-class taxonomy, our WIN cards fill in automatically. If he adds the CVaR cap to the headline, the gap closes. This audit is designed to shrink as the author propagates his API-level rigor to the visible layer. Two distinct closing mechanisms apply: some rows close automatically when API-level rendering catches up to a propagation lag (render-lag rows); others require an active edit to the HTML headline by the author (author-action rows). It will not shrink if he only edits the machine-readable files — which is the pattern we are documenting.
patches.replace: The Currently classified claims sub-table and the gap-audit table's six rows are the testable monitor for this section. If the dome author updates the predictions page to reconcile the 10 / 12 / 1 count drift, the gap closes. If he classifies more claims under the 5-class taxonomy, the sub-table grows and the “classifications stalled near 9” concern resolves. If he adds the CVaR cap to the headline, Row 5 closes. Two distinct closing mechanisms apply: some rows close automatically when API-level rendering catches up (render-lag rows); others require an active HTML edit by the author (author-action rows). This audit is designed to shrink as the author propagates his API-level rigor to the visible layer — it will not shrink if he only edits the machine-readable files, which is the pattern we are documenting.
patches.rationale: ISS-2030 (major): Canary paragraph falsely claimed WIN cards auto-populate from author_* fields — the author_* plan was abandoned in EXP-397 in favor of the sub-table. ISS-2032 (moderate): 10/9/1 count drift was stale; TLDR already shows 10/12/1.

patches.find: strict_prospective_confirmed = 1 in claim_index.jsonHomepage: 69 Confirmed, 94.5% accuracyThe author's strict definition, applied strictly, yields 1. The headline counts a different, looser population. Both numbers are true under their own definitions; only the headline is load-bearing.
patches.replace: claim_index.json: 1 entry tagged claim_class=="prospective_confirmed" (pred-eclipse-tier3)Homepage: 69 Confirmed, 94.5% accuracyThe dome's claim taxonomy, applied strictly, yields 1 prospective_confirmed entry. The headline counts a different, looser population. Both numbers are true under their own definitions; only the headline is load-bearing. Note: the earlier strict_prospective_confirmed field was removed in a later API restructure; the underlying count is preserved via claim_class tagging.
patches.rationale: ISS-2031 (major): strict_prospective_confirmed was removed from claim_index.json (API restructure, explicit in the Schema-drift note above). Row 1 citing it as a current live field contradicts the same section. Replace with the live mechanism (claim_class tagging).

patches.find: registry_integrity_notes, strict_prospective_confirmed, the 5-class claim taxonomy, the CVaR-based exclusion cap
patches.replace: registry_integrity_notes, the 5-class claim taxonomy (including claim_class tagging), the CVaR-based exclusion cap
patches.rationale: ISS-2031 (major): Kernel-of-truth credited strict_prospective_confirmed as honest methodology, but it was removed from the live API. Replaced with the live mechanism (claim_class tagging) per curmudgeon recommendation.

patches.find: live.html footer: "67 confirmed"Homepage: 69 confirmedTwo numbers for the same quantity on the same domain. Not a rounding or cutoff distinction — an unreconciled footer.
patches.rationale: ISS-2033 (moderate) + ISS-2029 (minor): live.html footer now shows 69 confirmed (same as homepage), closing this gap. Row 4 removed as resolved per curmudgeon recommendation option (a).

patches.find: Taxonomy currently applied to 9 of 69+ claims.
patches.replace: Taxonomy currently applied to 9 of 69 claims (using the dome's own 69-confirmed headline as the denominator).
patches.rationale: ISS-2035 (minor): 69+ is sloppy — either 69 (dome headline) or 71 (our review count). Using 69 is most defensible for an audit of the dome's own classification surface.

patches.replace:
Note on registry_integrity_notes: the dome's claim_index.json publishes two registry_integrity_notes entries — one covering the two WIN-058 claim keys (shared visible label), one covering WIN-062. Three rows are flagged "yes" because two separate claim keys both map to WIN-058.

Schema-drift note.
patches.rationale: ISS-2036 (minor): Add clarifying footnote explaining why 3 rows are flagged yes when there are only 2 registry_integrity_notes in the API.

patches.find: The dome's argument commits a false dilemma:
patches.replace: And even granting the dome a galaxy-scale physics it does not have, the argument structure remains a false dilemma:
patches.rationale: ISS-2027 (moderate): c5 hole 1 carry-forward. Prefixing with the concessive clause subordinates the false-dilemma point, preventing dome defenders from treating it as the primary attack vector. Exact text from c5.holes_found[1].recommendation.

patches.find: RAR has no dome-side derivation, only borrowed MOND symbols.
patches.replace: The dome's own published parameter table includes λ<sub>A</sub> = 4,283 Mpc — c/H₀ to 0.01% precision — making agnosticism about extragalactic geometry incoherent.
patches.rationale: ISS-2028 (minor): c5 hole 2 carry-forward. lambda_A=4,283 Mpc is the strongest self-contradiction argument and belongs in tldr_verdict, not buried in detail_extra.

patches.find: The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.
patches.replace: The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Note further: reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB — back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations (10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT). This implied band sits at or above the upper edge of the global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble (5–15 nT per Kim & Chang 2018, the same 207-measurement ensemble the dome itself invokes for WIN-068). A defender who assumes the dome takes a typical-ensemble ΔB of 10–13 nT for August 2026 finds that 10/1.677 ≈ 6.0 µGal and 13/1.677 ≈ 7.8 µGal — both below the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target. The arithmetic is internally consistent only if the dome commits to a ΔB premise above the typical ensemble; that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located. Either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB prediction in advance (in which case the κ derivation becomes auditable in advance — a symmetric strengthening of the vise) or it does not (in which case the 10–13 µGal target's derivation has an additional unspecified premise beyond κ). Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.
patches.rationale: EXP-384 Patch A: SEC-6.14 vise paragraph — adds back-calculation showing the 10-13 µGal SG-pulse target back-implies a ΔB premise of 16.8-21.8 nT, above the typical 5-15 nT ensemble range (Kim & Chang 2018). This strengthens the vise: dome either commits to an unpublished above-ensemble ΔB premise, or the derivation has an unspecified additional premise.
